sourceTitle: Bring your codebase into context with Notion's GitHub integration

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of linking a GitHub Pull Request property to a Notion database?

options.text: It automatically creates a new GitHub repository.
options.explanation: Linking a PR property does not create repositories; it connects existing PRs to Notion pages.

options.text: Updates to the PR sync instantly to the Notion page, keeping the team informed.
options.explanation: The integration ensures any changes in the linked PR appear in Notion in real‑time, providing up‑to‑date context.

options.text: It allows Notion to push code changes back to GitHub.
options.explanation: Sync is one‑way from GitHub to Notion; Notion cannot push code edits.

options.text: It converts the PR into a Notion task automatically.
options.explanation: The PR remains a PR; the property only links it, not transforms its type.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Magic Keywords is correct?

options.text: They can close a Notion task only when the PR is merged, if the keyword indicates a closing action.
options.explanation: Magic Keywords like “closes” or “finishes” trigger Notion to mark the linked task as done when the PR merges.

options.text: They create a new GitHub issue each time a Notion task is updated.
options.explanation: Magic Keywords link existing PRs to tasks; they do not generate new issues.

options.text: They are required for any GitHub‑Notion connection to work.
options.explanation: The integration works without Magic Keywords; they are optional shortcuts for automation.

options.text: They prevent any comments from syncing between GitHub and Notion.
options.explanation: Comments can still sync; Magic Keywords only affect linking and status changes.

questions.question: When enabling comment sync, what does turning on the "Activity Comments" toggle achieve?

options.text: It copies all GitHub comments into a separate Notion page unrelated to the PR.
options.explanation: Comments are added to the linked Notion page, not a separate page.

options.text: It mirrors GitHub comments as Notion page comments in real‑time.
options.explanation: Activating the toggle makes GitHub comments appear as comments on the associated Notion page instantly.

options.text: It disables all other GitHub integrations.
options.explanation: Comment sync is independent and does not affect other integration features.

options.text: It automatically resolves all open GitHub issues.
options.explanation: Comment sync does not change issue status; it only displays comments.

questions.question: Why are synced databases described as "one‑way only" in the context of the GitHub integration?

options.text: Because changes made in Notion will overwrite the GitHub repository.
options.explanation: The sync does not push changes back to GitHub; it only reflects GitHub data.

options.text: Because updates must originate in GitHub to appear in Notion.
options.explanation: Synced databases pull data from GitHub; any modifications need to happen in GitHub to be reflected.

options.text: Because Notion automatically creates new GitHub branches for each view.
options.explanation: No branches are created; the integration only reads data.

options.text: Because Notion cannot display GitHub data without a manual refresh.
options.explanation: Syncs happen automatically; the limitation is the direction of data flow, not refresh frequency.

questions.question: Which custom view configuration would best help a project manager track which team member is responsible for each open pull request?

options.text: Create a view filtered by the "Status" property showing only closed PRs.
options.explanation: Closed PRs are not relevant for current responsibility tracking.

options.text: Create a view filtered by the "Assignee" property showing only open PRs.
options.explanation: Filtering by Assignee and open status surfaces the PRs each person is currently working on.

options.text: Create a view sorted by creation date without any filters.
options.explanation: Sorting alone won’t isolate responsibility or open PRs.

options.text: Create a view that groups by the "Repository" property.
options.explanation: Grouping by repository doesn’t directly reveal who is assigned to each PR.

questions.question: How do database relations enhance the visibility of code changes across non‑technical teams?

options.text: They automatically generate code documentation from PR descriptions.
options.explanation: Relations link records; they don’t create documentation.

options.text: They allow you to connect PRs to project roadmaps, goals, and other databases, showing how code impacts broader objectives.
options.explanation: Relations bridge PRs with other data, giving stakeholders context on how changes align with goals.

options.text: They sync all comments from GitHub into a separate Notion page for each team.
options.explanation: Comments sync via the Activity Comments toggle, not via relations.

options.text: They replace the need for Magic Keywords entirely.
options.explanation: Relations and Magic Keywords serve different purposes; one doesn’t replace the other.

questions.question: What advantage does adding a GitHub link preview to a Notion document provide to stakeholders?

options.text: It allows stakeholders to edit the GitHub PR directly from Notion.
options.explanation: Link previews are read‑only; editing still occurs in GitHub.

options.text: It gives a visual snapshot of the PR or issue, letting stakeholders understand the work without leaving Notion.
options.explanation: Previews embed key information, reducing context‑switching for non‑technical viewers.

options.text: It automatically closes the PR when the Notion page is archived.
options.explanation: Archiving a Notion page has no effect on the PR status.

options.text: It duplicates the entire codebase into the Notion page.
options.explanation: Only a preview of the specific PR/issue appears, not the whole repository.

questions.question: Which scenario best illustrates the use of Notion AI to accelerate a non‑technical team's workflow with GitHub data?

options.text: A designer asks Notion AI for a summary of open mobile app issues to understand current blockers.
options.explanation: The AI can pull and summarize GitHub issues, giving the designer quick insight without digging into GitHub.

options.text: A developer uses Notion AI to automatically merge pull requests.
options.explanation: AI cannot perform merge actions; it only provides information.

options.text: A product manager asks Notion AI to rewrite the code of a feature.
options.explanation: AI does not edit code; it assists with queries and summaries.

options.text: A QA tester asks Notion AI to delete old GitHub branches.
options.explanation: Deletion of branches is an action outside AI's scope; AI only retrieves information.

questions.question: If a team wants to ensure that a Notion task is *not* marked as done when a linked PR is merged, what should they do?

options.text: Use a reference‑only link for that task instead of a standard Magic Keyword link.
options.explanation: Reference‑only links prevent the automatic status change while still maintaining the connection.

options.text: Turn off the GitHub integration entirely.
options.explanation: Disabling the integration would remove all syncing, not just the status change.

options.text: Add the word "ignore" to the PR description.
options.explanation: There is no built‑in keyword that stops the status update; the reference‑only option is the correct method.

options.text: Manually delete the task after the PR merges.
options.explanation: Deleting the task defeats the purpose of keeping the record linked.

questions.question: What is a key reason organizations might keep GitHub access limited to developers while using Notion for broader project updates?

options.text: Notion can replace the need for a version control system.
options.explanation: Notion does not manage code versions; GitHub remains the VCS.

options.text: It centralizes status and context in a tool non‑technical team members are already comfortable with.
options.explanation: Using Notion as the single source of truth reduces context switching for stakeholders who don’t use GitHub.

options.text: It automatically grants all employees write access to the codebase.
options.explanation: Access permissions remain controlled in GitHub; Notion does not alter them.

options.text: It eliminates the need for code reviews.
options.explanation: Code reviews still happen in GitHub; Notion only surfaces information.
